rulings.Case Title: FILAMENT LIGHTING, LLC VS. ARCHIPELAGO LIGHTING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Judgment Debtors Motion To Compel Judgment Creditor To File And Serve Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTs ARCHIPELAGO LIGHTING, INC., DM LIGHTING TECHNOLOGIES, INC., VICTOR DENG, and ARCHIPELAGO LIGHTING TEC, INC. (Judgment Debtors') Motion To Compel Judgment Creditor To File And Serve Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment Judgment Debtors' Archipelago Lighting, Inc., DM Lighting Technologies, Inc., Archipelago Lighting Tec, Inc., and Victor Deng's motion to compel judgment creditor to file and serve on judgment debtors' counsel an acknowledgement of satisfaction of judgment, or, in the alternative, for an order directing the clerk of this court to enter satisfaction of the judgment and for penalties and attorneys' fees, is denied. The record is undisputed that judgment creditor Filament Lighting, LLC did not become aware of judgment debtors' unauthorized wire transfers to judgment creditor's law firm's general operating account until March 13, 2019, five days after they were made. (Albrecht Decl., 15.) Through counsel, judgment creditor promptly rejected the second wire transfer on the ground that it was insufficient to satisfy the amount of the judgment in full. Judgment Debtors' reliance on Gray1 CPB, LLC v. SCC Acquisitions, Inc. (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 882 is misplaced, since there the court found that "[t]he judgment was fully satisfied when [the judgment creditor] accepted the cashier's check." (Id. at 894.) Here, in contrast, Filament did not accept the second wire transfer. "Even if payment is by certified check or similar instrument, the acceptance of which arguably constitutes satisfaction [citation], the judgment creditor retains, at the least, the option of rejecting the certified check and filing the motion or memorandum for enforcement costs and fees." (Conservatorship of McQueen (2014) 59 Cal.4th 602, 615 [emphasis in original].) That is just what Filament did here. Thus, the judgment was not fully satisfied before Filament filed its March 8, 2019 memorandum of costs. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 685.070, 724.010(c) ["Where a money judgment is satisfied by payment to the judgment creditor by check or other form of noncash payment that is to be honored upon presentation by the judgment creditor for payment, the obligation of the judgment creditor to give or file an acknowledgement of satisfaction of judgment arises only when the check or other form of noncash payment has actually been honored upon presentation for payment."].) The Court reserves ruling as to the amounts claimed in the March 8, 2019 memorandum of costs, which is the subject of Judgment Debtors' motion to strike set for hearing on April 26, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JEREMY L. FRIEDMAN VS. MEDHANIE BERHE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Entry Of Voluntary Dismissal And Withdrawal Of Request For Trial De Novo As Moot
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JEREMY FRIEDMAN's Motion For Entry Of Voluntary Dismissal And Withdrawal Of Request For Trial De Novo As Moot Plaintiff Jeremy L. Friedman's motion for entry of voluntary dismissal and withdrawal of request for trial de novo as moot is unopposed. However, no court order is required or warranted. If Plaintiff wishes to withdraw his request for a trial de novo, he may file a request for voluntary dismissal of the action. (Code Civ. Proc. § 581(b)(1).) Alternatively, if Plaintiff wishes to condition the dismissal on particular terms, he may seek the other parties' written consent and submit a stipulation and order to that effect. (Code Civ. Proc. § 581(b)(2).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BRETT ALMIROL VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Relief, Ccp Sec. 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BRETT ALMIROL's Motion For Relief, CCP Sec. 664.6 Plaintiff Brett Almirol's "Motion for Relief, CCP Sec. 664.6" is denied. In violation of the Code of Civil Procedure and the Rules of Court, Plaintiff's moving papers do not contain a memorandum of points and authorities, a supporting declaration, a copy of the settlement agreement Plaintiff claims was entered into on January 31, 2018, or even a summary of its terms. They are woefully inadequate to meet Plaintiff's burden to show entitlement to any relief. In addition, Defendant Total Renal Care, Inc.'s showing establishes that Plaintiff is improperly requesting that the Court alter the terms of the parties' settlement agreement, rather than enforce it pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, and that Plaintiff has failed to comply with his obligations under that agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. rulings.Case Title: ALAN MOSS VS. STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTO-MOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Defendant State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company's motion to set aside default is granted. "The court may, upon any terms as may be just, relieve a party or his or her legal representative from a judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against him or her through his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (Code Civ. Proc. § 473(b).) Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) is liberally construed to prevent injustice and allow cases to be decided on their merits. (Frank E. Beckett Co. v. Bobbit (1960) 180 Cal.App.2d Supp. 921, 924.) State Farm has explained that it was actively investigating Plaintiff's uninsured motorist claim that had been submitted to arbitration and that the arbitration caption was similar to this litigation. (Struble Decl. 6.) Therefore, State Farm's claims department mistakenly thought the complaint pertained to the uninsured motorist claim and did not forward it to defense counsel until the default judgment was later discovered. (Id. 7-8.) Even where a default is attributable to a neglect of duty, such mistakes may nevertheless be excusable for purposes of Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) where the other party was not prejudiced. (See Benjamin v. Dalmo Mfg. Co. (1948) 31 Cal.2d 523, 527, 531 [holding that affidavits from a company president and secretary explaining their failure to read or comprehend a summons constituted excusable neglect].) Plaintiff has made no showing that he will be prejudiced by the Court setting aside this default. The Court declines to rule on Defendant's evidentiary objections as they are not germane to its ruling on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for State Farm is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BALANCE STUDIO, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION DBA VS. CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC A CALIFORNIA ENTITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Cybernet Entertainment LLC's demurrer to the complaint filed by plaintiff Balance Studio Inc. is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract, sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, and sustained without leave to amend as to the third and fourth causes of action for negligent misrepresentation and for preliminary and permanent injunction. Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged a cause of action for breach of contract because plaintiff's interpretation of the two prohibitions in para. 4, read in conjunction with para. 5 of the agreement, is not unreasonable and should be accepted by the court on demurrer. "'So long as the pleading does not place a clearly erroneous construction upon the provisions of the contract, in passing upon the sufficiency of the complaint, we must accept as correct plaintiff's allegations as to the meaning of the agreement.'" (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1385; see also Fire Ins. Exchange v. Superior Court (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 446, 452 ["Ordinarily, a general demurrer does not lie as to a portion of a cause of action, and if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled."].) The fraudulent misrepresentation/promissory fraud cause of action is not barred by the economic loss rule. (See Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 551-552 ["conduct amounting to a breach of contract becomes tortious only when it also violates a duty independent of the contract arising from principles of tort law. . . Tort damages have been permitted in contract cases . . . where the contract was fraudulently induced."]). The demurer is sustained because plaintiff has failed to allege promissory fraud with the requisite specificity. (See Rossberg v. Bank of America, N.A. (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1481, 1498 [each element of a promissory fraud claim must be alleged with particularity]; Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157 ["The requirement of specificity in a fraud action against a corporation requires the plaintiff to allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written."].) Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to cure that deficiency. The negligent misrepresentation cause of action is not supported by the allegations, which do not refer to past or present statements of fact but only to a false promise. There is no cause of action for a negligent false promise. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 159.) Because plaintiff has not shown how amendment will cure that deficiency, the demurrer to this cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Because plaintiff has not opposed defendant's demurrer to the cause of action for injunctive relief, and because injunctive relief is a remedy and not a cause of action in itself, the demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches the report and recommendation and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BALANCE STUDIO, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION DBA VS. CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC A CALIFORNIA ENTITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To File Plaintiffs ***Unredacted*** Opposition To Defendants Demurrer Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 11, PLAINTIFF BALANCE STUDIO, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION DBA Motion To File Plaintiffs ***Unredacted*** Opposition To Defendants Demurrer Under Seal PLAINTIFF BALANCE STUDIO, INC.'s motion to file unredacted opposition to defendant's demurrer under seal is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown pursuant to California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN STEUER ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD's Motion To Stay Defendant California Franchise Tax Board's motion to stay action pending the Court of Appeal's decision in Paula Trust v. California Franchise Tax Board, No. A154691 (First App. Dist., Div. 3), is granted. The action is stayed until the Court of Appeal's decision in that case becomes final without either party filing a timely petition for review in the California Supreme Court or, if such a petition for review is filed, until the Supreme Court has acted on the petition for review. If review is granted, either party may request the Court to continue the stay. rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO PANIAGUA ET AL VS. MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANTs MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC, BEAR BRUIN VENTURES, INC., WILLIAM STUART, and CAROLYN STUART's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is off calendar. On March 14, 2019, Defendants appealed this court's order that denied their motion to compel arbitration. That appeal divests this court of jurisdiction per Code of Civil Procedure § 916. "[A]n appeal from the denial of a motion to compel arbitration automatically stays all further trial court proceedings on the merits." (Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 190.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MENDOZA VS. ROSENDA RODRIGUEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Rosa Mendoza To Appear And Testify At Deposition And To Produce Documents; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Rosa Mendoza And Or Julio J. Ramos In The Sum Of $2,710 And Or Terminating Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Proposed Order; Declaration Of Patrick Hill
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT ROSENDA RODRIGUEZ'S Motion For Order Compelling Rosa Mendoza To Appear And Testify At Deposition And To Produce Documents; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Rosa Mendoza And Or Julio J. Ramos In The Sum Of $2,710 And Or Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
